item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The first image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0), as it can be found here under All Rights Reserved. The second image was created for the article and thus is cc-compliant.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: That works.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Perhaps making the add-on paragraph as the very last collapsible will maintain the article's own story (and give readers additional readings to work on, after going through 1393).

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I agree with the extra reference to 2493, and making the link a separate paragraph, but since the fact that this is an extradimensional disposal service is kind of the big reveal at the final addenda, it might be better to have that final paragraph at the end of the article, or in its own collapsible. Maybe.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
I feel that the following ought to be a paragraph on its own, since the sudden direct reference to another SCP might be disjoint with the flow of the article.

Similarities between the nature of SCP-1393 and SCP-364 have been noted, and the possibility of a shared origin is currently under investigation.

I am also considering the possibility of SCP-2493, which also involves extra-dimensional trash disposal and collection (albeit with antimatter involved). If we factor that in, we can have something like:

Similarities between SCP-1393 and SCP-364 have been noted. Factoring in SCP-2493-1's account, it suggests the possibility of extra-dimensional disposal of materials as a common practice.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

It was mentioned up thread that this article has similarities to SCP-364 in that both appear to involve the dumping of materials through a portal (though 364 appears to have less collateral damage with respect to human civilization.) It is possible that both may be owned by the same extradimensional company as is seen in this article. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Before SCP-1393 entered its current active state, a humanoid being designated SCP-1393-1 was observed performing reconnaissance and surveying of the immediate area. SCP-1393-1 was reported wearing a Level A/Type 1 Hazmat suit equipped with a polarized face shield and a SCBA unit. The Foundation was contacted about the event and SCP-1393-1 was interviewed about its actions and purpose. To date, SCP-1393-1 has not been seen since SCP-1393 activated.

To this:

Before SCP-1393 entered its current active state, a humanoid being designated SCP-1393-1 was observed performing reconnaissance and surveying of the immediate area. SCP-1393-1 was reported wearing a Level A/Type 1 Hazmat suit equipped with a polarized face shield and a SCBA unit. The Foundation was contacted about the event and SCP-1393-1 was interviewed about its actions and purpose. To date, SCP-1393-1 has not been seen since SCP-1393 activated. Similarities between the nature of SCP-1393 and SCP-364 have been noted, and the possibility of a shared origin is currently under investigation.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason I can't unsee this guy from another dimension who's in a hurry as Rick from Rick and Morty. Hilarious. Up voted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The business card's image placement is a little weird but other than that this is pretty cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me of the creeper-world games, although here the creep is just molten garbage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't like it. Probably because I just read Dmatix' Universal Dumping Ground SCP, and the fact that Ionian Drop Point is one of my favourites. The interview log reminds me of the Llama delivery, and that Door to Door salesman guy's interviews, which are in my opinion the worst parts of those articles. "Alien dude in a rush brushing of Foundation guy because they're busy". Meh.

Though I'll give you points for using the photo in an article that it works. And it's technically well written. I just really don't like how its pulled off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, this is nice. I can't wait when the main picture rumbles somebody's jimbos "because it looks like from a PC game", but the thing I like the most is the originality, with the business card and such. Earth as one of the universe's dumpster. Nice. +1

EDIT: God damn it, I guess I won't lose the "231 syndrome" anytime soon. Just looking at the phone number made me think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Talk about your spoiler alerts. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh. I was reading that slag mix as something along the lines of the elements/minerals that make up the Earth. Which, if smelt slag, would be true enough. Still, I prefer to interpret it as they're unintentionally making new planets in various alt-U's as a side effect of waste dumping.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article made me smile. That, along with the reasons many other people have mentioned, earns you an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To clarify…"silica, quicklime, alumina, sodium borate, and magnesite" aren't random minerals chosen out of thin air. They are all by-products of metal ore smelting processes. The other 15% in my mind is not relevant enough to mention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
VistaPrint: So cheap and easy1 that even transdimensional corporations use it!

Footnotes
1. Like your MOM! Bam!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ha! When I saw that, I thought "Extraterrestrials use VistaPrint?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An alternate reality using ours as a dumpster… I was kind of iffy at first but then I read the interview log. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Roughly 85% of this material is primarily composed of a combination of …" is word salad. What about the other 15 (or so) percent?

I'm on the fence about the concept in general, may come back and read it again later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think there's a typo on the card. …"Managment"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: By any chance was this inspired by portal 2's multiverse test chamber thing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is silly, but the right kind of silly. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh. Felt like the business card was supposed to sell it for me, and it really didn't (mostly because of the fact that it feels like it's out of a DC comic). The idea didn't interest me either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The interview reminds me a lot of that one SCP in the… graveyard? I can't remember. I might be drunk.

Anyway, this is fun and the business card is a great touch. I particularly like the "psi-xi-delta" in the phone (???) number.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THAT'S what I was thinking of. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks be to VistaPrint.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much better lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I might. See if this new image is any better?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Toss me a pm if you'd like; wouldn't mind helping out with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not the best at design, I'll admit. That image may get replaced.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh. I really like this one. The business card looks kinda cheesy, though… Sorry, Design Theory and Color Theory classes nagging in the back of my head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope. That's 100% real. I believe it's from the Blair Athol coal mine in Australia.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me a bit of SCP-364.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's the source of that picture? To me it looks like it's from a video game or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea that there's some intergalactic organisation whose sole responsibility is dumping their rubbish onto our Earth is an interesting one.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …an alternate reality is using ours as a dumpster?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm.

I feel like I'm missing something. This one really isn't grabbing me. -1, may come back and take another look at it later.
